summary: PR 2587 is a focused item/fighter decomp-matching cleanup for Link Arrow, centered on matching itLinkarrow_UnkMotion1_Anim and renaming the 0x802A850C Link Arrow helper from it_802A850C to itLinkArrow_802A850C across symbols, header, implementation, and fighter call sites. The diff also replaces some raw object access with GET_ITEM/GET_JOBJ, improves local names, and adjusts stack padding/local layout in itlinkarrow.c. The only loaded bot report says GALE01 had no changes; there are no human review comments in the slice.

key_files.role: Primary implementation change for Link Arrow matching: renamed helper/function identifiers, cleaned locals, used GET_ITEM/GET_JOBJ in touched code, and adjusted padding/local layout around itLinkarrow_UnkMotion1_Anim.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: src/melee/it/items/itlinkarrow.c hunks around 0x802A850C and itLinkarrow_UnkMotion1_Anim

key_files.role: Header prototype updated from it_802A850C to itLinkArrow_802A850C.
Symbol table rename for .text:0x802A850C from it_802A850C to itLinkArrow_802A850C.
Link fighter call site updated to the renamed Link Arrow helper.
Kirby copied-Link special call site updated to the renamed Link Arrow helper.

what_changed.text: Renamed the global function at .text:0x802A850C from it_802A850C to itLinkArrow_802A850C in symbols.txt, the item header, the item implementation, and both fighter call sites.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftLink and ftKirby call-site hunks

what_changed.text: Renamed related inline helpers from it_802A850C_inline / it_802A850C_inline_2 to itLinkArrow_802A850C_inline / itLinkArrow_802A850C_inline_2.
Cleaned local variable names in the touched Link Arrow code, including var_r30 to jobj and sp60 to flags.
In itLinkarrow_UnkMotion1_Anim-related code, changed Item access from gobj->user_data to GET_ITEM(gobj) and JObj access in one inline from gobj->hsd_obj to GET_JOBJ(gobj).
evidence_refs: raw_diff: src/melee/it/items/itlinkarrow.c itLinkarrow_UnkMotion1_Anim_inline and itLinkarrow_UnkMotion1_Anim hunks

what_changed.text: Adjusted stack-padding representation around itLinkarrow_UnkMotion1_Anim by replacing PAD_STACK(16) with explicit u8 pad[4] and u8 _pad[4] locals in the displayed hunk.
Loaded bot issue comment reports: 'Report for GALE01 ... No changes'.

smart_moves.text: The symbol rename was propagated consistently across symbol metadata, the public item header, implementation name, inline helper names, and known Link/Kirby call sites in the same PR.
The touched function body used existing object access helpers where possible, replacing raw user_data with GET_ITEM and one raw hsd_obj access with GET_JOBJ.
Local names were made more reviewable without over-renaming unknown globals: jobj and flags replace generated-looking var_r30 and sp60 while other offset-based item fields remain unchanged.

decomp_lessons.lesson: For an address-style item function that becomes understood as belonging to a specific item, a scoped item-prefix rename can be reviewable when the PR updates all visible declarations, symbols, and call sites together.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: it_802A850C renamed to itLinkArrow_802A850C in symbols.txt, itlinkarrow.h, itlinkarrow.c, ftLk_SpecialN.c, and ftKb_SpecialNPk.c

decomp_lessons.lesson: Accessor macros such as GET_ITEM and GET_JOBJ can be part of matching cleanup when they express the same object access in touched item animation code.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: itLinkarrow_UnkMotion1_Anim_inline uses GET_JOBJ; itLinkarrow_UnkMotion1_Anim uses GET_ITEM

decomp_lessons.lesson: Stack-shape changes in matching work may appear as explicit small padding arrays rather than PAD_STACK, but the PR slice does not explain why this exact layout was required.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: PAD_STACK(16) replaced with u8 pad[4] and u8 _pad[4]

naming_conventions.convention: Use the item-specific prefix itLinkArrow_ for the function at 0x802A850C once it is identified as Link Arrow logic.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: symbols.txt and itlinkarrow.h rename to itLinkArrow_802A850C

naming_conventions.convention: Prefer role-based local names such as jobj and flags over generated-looking names such as var_r30 and sp60 when the local role is evident from calls and argument position.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: var_r30 -> jobj in HSD_JObjLoadJoint helper; sp60 -> flags in it_8026EA20 call

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Synchronized symbol/header/call-site rename for a matched function while preserving its address and size metadata.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: config/GALE01/symbols.txt keeps .text:0x802A850C size:0x478 while changing name
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_or_note: The slice supports the rename mechanics but does not include the reasoning source for the semantic name beyond the item file and title.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use accessor macros in touched code to express object payload and JObj retrieval.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_or_note: Consistent with typed/accessor cleanup standards, but no objdiff details are shown beyond the bot report.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Replace a broad PAD_STACK macro with explicit small padding locals to shape stack layout.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_or_note: This is a matching tactic that normally needs verifier evidence; loaded evidence only provides the diff and a bot 'No changes' report.

review_feedback.text: No human review comments are present in the loaded slice.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl: only PR body and decomp-dev[bot] issue comment loaded

review_feedback.text: Bot report for GALE01 says 'No changes'.

follow_up_queries: Find other PRs renaming address-style item functions to item-prefixed names with synchronized symbols/header/call-site updates.
Search for itlinkarrow.c PRs that replaced gobj->user_data or gobj->hsd_obj with GET_ITEM/GET_JOBJ.
Search for PRs where PAD_STACK was replaced with explicit u8 pad locals in matched item animation functions.
Search for itLinkarrow_UnkMotion1_Anim follow-up PRs or comments explaining the stack layout.

evidence_refs: metadata_json: PR metadata, changed file counts, merged_at
raw_diff: full 7144-byte diff for five files
text_corpus.jsonl: empty PR body and bot 'No changes' issue comment
notes: The diff is complete and supports file-level and code-change claims.
The PR body is empty, so author intent and rationale for specific matching choices are not available.
There are no loaded human review comments.
The bot report says 'No changes', but the slice does not include detailed build, objdiff, or checkdiff logs.
Stack-padding conclusions are weak because the diff shows the transformation but not the verification reason.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Propagate evidence-backed item function renames across symbols, headers, implementation, and call sites
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2587 shows a focused rename of the Link Arrow helper at 0x802A850C from it_802A850C to itLinkArrow_802A850C across config/GALE01/symbols.txt, src/melee/it/items/itlinkarrow.h, src/melee/it/items/itlinkarrow.c, and Link/Kirby call sites. This is a candidate example of keeping symbol metadata and prototypes synchronized during matching cleanup.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Use local role names and object accessors in matched item code when the role is clear
accepted_candidate_records.text: In the touched Link Arrow code, generated-looking locals were changed to jobj and flags, and raw object access was replaced with GET_ITEM/GET_JOBJ in the animation path. This is a candidate example aligned with conservative naming and typed/accessor cleanup.

source_update_candidates.title: itlinkarrow.c owns Link Arrow item helper at 0x802A850C
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2587 renamed the function at .text:0x802A850C from it_802A850C to itLinkArrow_802A850C in config/GALE01/symbols.txt and updated src/melee/it/items/itlinkarrow.h/c plus Link and Kirby call sites. Curator may consider recording this as a path/symbol fact if not already present.
source_update_candidates.reason: This is symbol/path metadata rather than a global decomp standard; it should be curator-reviewed before promotion.

rejection_notes: Do not promote the explicit pad-array change as a general standard; the PR slice lacks rationale and detailed objdiff evidence.
Do not infer human reviewer approval rationale; no human review comments are loaded.
Do not generalize itLinkArrow capitalization beyond the symbols shown in this PR without broader naming evidence.
